judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent loaded paper texts and citation records, then in execute_python identified 'food' papers by simply checking if the word "food" appears anywhere in the full text (instead of extracting the explicit domain field), merged on title, and summed citations, causing an incorrect set selection and the wrong total (876 vs. 11203).
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After querying both databases, the agent’s final execute_python call (function-call-3592722655941463510) tried to extract a "domain:" line from each paper’s text using a regex and then filter for 'food', which produced no matches and thus a 0 citation sum, indicating an incorrect implementation of domain extraction from the correct data sources.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent’s execute_python step labeled papers as ‘food’ domain by naively checking if the word “food” appears anywhere in the full text (from paper_docs) instead of extracting the domain metadata, leading to an incomplete join with Citations and an undercounted total (876) rather than the correct 11203.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent’s execute_python code labeled a paper as 'food' domain only if the word "food" appeared anywhere in the full text (if 'food' in text.lower()), rather than extracting and matching the domain metadata, causing an incorrect set of papers to be merged with citations and resulting in an undercount (876 instead of 11203).
